segments.text: Man: Good morning.
Man: Ken's Appliance.
Man: Can I help you?
Woman: Yes.
Woman: I'm ringing about my fridge.
Woman: It's not working at the moment.
Man: I'd like to find out a few more details, if I may.
Woman: Yes, sure.
Man: First I need to make sure if your fridge is still covered by warranty.
Woman: Oh, right.
Woman: I bought it just last week and it originally came with 2 years of warranty coverage, but I've extended the period to 3 years.
Woman: So it's definitely under warranty.
Man: Right.
Man: Now, what model is it?
Woman: Let me take a look … It should be somewhere on this warranty card.
Woman: Oh, here, on the top right-hand corner.
Woman: It's a Top Mount.
Man: And the colour of the fridge?
Woman: It's a silver one.
Woman: It is really modern and perfectly matches the whole décor of my kitchen.
Man: I've made a note of that.
Man: So can you tell me the exact date you purchased the fridge?
Woman: I remember it was delivered on January 15th.
Woman: I must have bought it a couple of days earlier.
Woman: Right, it was on the 12th.
Man: OK.
Man: Then what is the problem with the fridge?
Woman: Oh, it's a complete disaster.
Woman: First there is something wrong with the alarm.
Woman: I mean it is beeping continuously, even when the door is shut.
Woman: It's really annoying.
Woman: And there's also a problem with its temperature control.
Woman: It's now 10 degrees, but I set it to be minus 8.
Woman: It seemed to be working fine for the first couple of days.
Man: Sorry about that, but rest assured, Madam.
Man: We'll fix it all.
Woman: Where is your maintenance centre?
Man: Ken's Appliance is located on Elm Street, just a few steps from the station.
Man: And, did the broken fridge cause any loss to you?
Woman: Yes, actually it was used to store food for my sandwich shop.
Man: Any idea of the total value of loss?
Woman: Let me make an estimate here.
Woman: The vegetables in the fridge cost me around $50, and the meat cost more than $100.
Woman: So the overall loss is about $180.
Woman: I didn't notice the abnormal temperature until something inside went bad and the whole fridge smelled terrible.
Woman: I had to toss everything away.
Woman: Luckily, no one ate the food inside and got sick.
Man: That's a relief.
Woman: But the damage has really caused me great inconvenience.
Woman: I've nowhere to put the raw materials and thus could not open for business these days.
Woman: The longer it takes to fix the fridge, the more it will cost me.
Woman: You know … I have a whole bunch of bills to pay, like water, electricity, rent …
Man: Don't worry, we'll attend to your problems as soon as possible.
Woman: I need to know precisely when you can send a guy to fix all the problems.
Woman: It is very urgent.
Woman: Is there anyone who can keep me posted on the latest information?
Man: How about this?
Man: I'll have the manager ring you later.
Man: Does that work for you?
Woman: OK … I'll be expecting his call then.
Woman: Do you know what exactly is wrong with the fridge?
Man: For your case, we assume it's the door that has to be fixed.
Man: The abnormal temperature and the noisy alarm both indicate that.
Woman: Yes, indeed.
Woman: I've just noticed that the door is dented.
Man: It may have to be replaced then.
Man: But we'll have to check the availability of our repair guy first.
Man: We'll phone you back later.
Woman: OK, thank you.
Man: You're welcome.
Man: Bye.
